---
name: "GSD-Review-Plan"
agent: agent
description: "Use when you need to review and fix a planning markdown created by the plan-tasks prompt, with focus on logic, structure, crypto correctness, completeness, and checklist quality."
argument-hint: "current_plan=<path> [review_goal=\"<target task or scope>\"]"
---

# Review Plan Tasks

Review and fix the planning markdown generated by the `plan-tasks` prompt.

## Input

- `current_plan`: full or relative path to the markdown document created by `plan-tasks`.
- `review_goal` (optional): short description of the exact task, scope, or section that the plan is expected to prioritize.

If `current_plan` is missing or ambiguous, stop and resolve that first.

## Mission

Read `current_plan`, and `review_goal` when it is provided, as a crypto analyst and spec reviewer.

Be critical, constructive, and objective.
Focus on critical blockers, obvious planning mistakes, and unresolved ambiguity before style-level concerns.
Own the review end to end: inspect, triage, fix, re-check, and finish only when the plan is execution-ready or blocked by an explicitly stated open decision.

Verify whether the document has any logical errors, unresolved pitfalls, structural inconsistencies, weak checklist items, missing acceptance rules, or cryptographic mistakes.

Fix all found issues directly in `current_plan` in YOLO mode.

> [!Important]
> Judge the plan by execution readiness, not by prose quality alone. A polished document still fails review if sequencing, acceptance, or checklist targets remain vague.

## Required Review Scope

Verify all of the following:

- logical correctness;
- internal consistency;
- structure and type integrity;
- cryptographic correctness;
- completeness of implementation guidance;
- checklist quality and full checklist compliance;
- correctness of file, crate, module, API, and trait integration guidance;
- quality of unit-test and integration-test planning;
- verifier flow and write flow correctness;
- absence of gray zones or unresolved choices;
- document quality for planning and acceptance use.

## Required Review Process

1. Read the full `current_plan` before editing.
2. Study the existing codebase before approving file targets, APIs, traits, or structural recommendations.
3. Run the `/crypto-architect` skill and treat its output as mandatory review evidence, not as optional guidance.
4. Run the `/security-audit` skill and treat its output as mandatory review evidence, not as optional guidance.
5. Extract the concrete cryptographic risks, invariants, misuse cases, and required corrections from `/crypto-architect` and `/security-audit` before deciding whether the context is sound.
6. Triage findings by severity: execution correctness, requirement coverage, dependency safety, cryptographic risk, and source ambiguity first; minor wording issues last.
7. Identify vague wording, missing constraints, weak sequencing, missing invariants, missing rejection rules, and poor codebase fit.
8. Check that canonical data forms, file targets, APIs, flows, tests, measurable completion conditions, and acceptance conditions are explicit where needed.
9. Verify that each major in-scope requirement, constraint, or workflow-goal element is covered by at least one concrete section or checklist item.
10. Verify that checklist items identify concrete execution targets when those targets materially affect implementation.
11. Rewrite weak sections so the document can be used for execution and acceptance without guessing.
12. Strengthen or expand checklist items when they are too abstract to drive implementation.
13. Add or fix Mermaid diagrams when the current structure or flow is still ambiguous.
14. If a referenced crate, file, module, type, or API target cannot be verified from the current codebase, relabel it explicitly as proposed rather than as an existing fact.
15. Use the `/doublecheck` skill in one-shot mode on the full review evidence, including corrected plan content and the findings derived from `/crypto-architect` and `/security-audit`.
16. Extract all verifiable claims and run the full three-layer verification pipeline.
17. Resolve or explicitly report every material issue raised by `/doublecheck` before treating the review as complete.
18. Edit `current_plan` directly until all found in-scope issues are resolved and the final review position is consistent with both skill outputs.

## Review Rules

- Review the document itself, not implementation code.
- The `/crypto-architect` ,`/security-audit`and `/doublecheck` steps are mandatory execution gates, not recommendations.
- Do not present a final review verdict, approval, or completion claim unless both skill runs were executed and their material findings were incorporated.
- Build the review, fixes, and final judgment on the results of both skill runs.
- If either skill is unavailable, incomplete, or returns unresolved material risk, fail closed and report the exact blocker instead of soft-approving the plan.
- Preserve the intended architecture unless the document is inconsistent or incorrect.
- Replace vague statements with normative wording such as "must", "must not", and "use".
- Keep the text concise, but do not leave planning gaps.
- Prefer exact names, exact data shapes, exact APIs, and exact ordering when the document can define them.
- Do not silently resolve material ambiguity in the plan or its source constraints; label it as an assumption, blocker, or open decision.
- Make reasonable assumptions only when they do not materially change task order or implementation shape.
- Base corrections on the existing codebase before allowing any new structure.
- Do not approve duplicated code paths or parallel abstractions when the current codebase can be extended.
- Before accepting a new structure, verify that no suitable existing structure already exists with a different signature.
- State clearly which crate and file each important change belongs to when the document is missing that information.
- Do not leave placeholder checklist items or fake subtasks.

> [!Caution]
> Do not tighten a weak section by inventing unverifiable APIs, file targets, or data shapes. If the source or codebase does not support a detail, label it as an assumption, blocker, or proposed target.

## Mandatory Context

You must read and follow these files before and during the review:

- [.github/requirements/Z00Z_DESIGN_FOUNDATION.md](../requirements/Z00Z_DESIGN_FOUNDATION.md)
- [.github/copilot-instructions.md](../copilot-instructions.md)

All requirements in those files must be satisfied.

## Completion Criteria

Completion requires all of these:

- `current_plan` was reviewed end to end;
- `/crypto-architect` was run and its material findings were integrated into the review and fixes;
- `/security-audit` was run and its material findings were integrated into the review and fixes;
- `/doublecheck` was run on the review evidence and all material verification issues were resolved or explicitly blocked;
- all found in-scope issues were fixed in the original document;
- planning, verifier, and write-flow guidance is explicit where needed;
- document-level ambiguities that can be fixed were removed;
- any remaining material ambiguity is explicitly labeled as an assumption, blocker, or open decision.

## Output Style

- Give short, direct updates.
- Present findings first.
- Prefer evidence-backed findings with exact plan or codebase references.
- State which file was reviewed and fixed.
- State what was fixed in `current_plan`.
- If no substantive issues are found, state that explicitly.
- State whether any source ambiguity could not be resolved from the document context.

## Non-Negotiable Rules

- Do not leave found document issues unresolved.
- Do not keep gray-zone wording when the document can prescribe a correct shape.
- Do not switch into implementation code changes.
- Do not review only parts of the document; review the full input file.
- Do not waste time on low-value nitpicks while critical planning issues remain.
- Follow all instructions above without exception.

## Example Invocation

`/review-plan-tasks current_plan=specs/014-z00z-storage/jmt-migration-spec.md`
